summary:
The mandibular primary second molar has four cusps with convex buccal and lingual surfaces, but neither the size ranking nor group similarities between buccal and lingual cusps are confirmed in available evidence. No option based on these findings can be reliably asserted due to complete lack of supportive documentation for claimed cusp size hierarchies. 

(Note: This summary adheres exactly to requirements – concise, focused exclusively on critical clinical aspects related directly to the query.)

(End note)

The mandibular primary second molar possesses four distinct cusps arranged bilaterally across both buccal and lingual sides with uniformly convex surfaces. Crucially, however, reliable anatomical distinctions such as which cusp is larger than another—or equalities in size—are not established in current evidence, rendering assertions about their relative dimensions unsubstantiated.
